zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander Williamson於1497年出生在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福時，他的父親John Williamson22歲，母親Jane Angevine20歲。他於1523年與Constance Alex在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1580年在他的家鄉享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alexander Williamson于1497年出生在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福时，他的父亲John Williamson22岁，母亲Jane Angevine20岁。他于1523年与Constance Alex在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1580年在他的家乡享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Williamson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northern) and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name William + patronymic -son ‘son of William’. This surname is also found in Northern Ireland (Ulster), especially in Antrim.
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German Wilhelmsen , Swedish Wilhelmsson, or Dutch and North German Willemsen , all cognates of 1.

History: This surname was brought to North America from northern England and northern Ireland independently by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. Timothy Williamson is recorded in Plymouth Colony in 1643.

variants.name: Willamson
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Williamson  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander Williamson was born in 1497, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England, his father, John Williamson, was 22 and his mother, Jane Angevine, was 20. He married Constance Alex in 1523, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1580, in his hometown, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander Williamson est né en 1497, à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John Williamson, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Jane Angevine, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Constance Alex en 1523, à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1580, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alexander Williamson 1497 in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Williamson 22 und seine Mutter Jane Angevine 20. Er hat 1523 in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England Constance Alex geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1580 im Alter von 83 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alexander Williamson è nato il nel 1497 nel Wilmslow, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, John Williamson, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Jane Angevine, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Constance Alex nel 1523, nel Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1580, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 83 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Williamson(이)가 1497년 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Williamson(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Angevine(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1523년 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 Constance Alex(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1580년 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alexander Williamson nasceu em 1497, em Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Williamson, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Jane Angevine, tinha 20 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alexander Williamson родился в 1497 г. в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Williamson было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Jane Angevine, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance Alex в 1523 г. в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1580 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет.
